title: তথ্য অধিকার আইন, ২০০৯
description: The Right to Information Act 2009 guarantees the right of every citizen of Bangladesh to obtain information from public authorities, subject to limited exceptions in the public interest. The Act establishes the Information Commission as the independent oversight body responsible for promoting and protecting this right, and creates the framework of obligations applicable to all public authorities regarding disclosure, record management, and response to information requests.

provisions.title: Section 1. Short Title and Commencement
provisions.content: This Act may be called the Right to Information Act, 2009. It extends to the whole of Bangladesh. It shall come into force on such date as the Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, appoint. The Act establishes the right of citizens to access information held by public authorities, implementing the constitutional guarantee of freedom of thought, conscience, and speech recognised in Article 39 of the Constitution of Bangladesh. Access to information held by public authorities is recognised as essential to democratic governance, public accountability, and the protection of other human rights.

provisions.title: Section 2. Definitions
provisions.content: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires: 'Information' means any material in any form, including records, documents, memos, e-mails, opinions, advices, press releases, circulars, orders, logbooks, contracts, reports, papers, samples, models, data material held in any electronic form and information relating to any private body which can be accessed by a public authority under any other law for the time being in force; 'Right to information' means the right to access information held by or under the control of a public authority; 'Public authority' means any authority or body or institution of self-government established or constituted by or under the Constitution, or by any other law made by Parliament, or by notification issued or order made by the appropriate Government, including any body owned, controlled or substantially financed by funds provided directly or indirectly by the appropriate Government; 'Information Commission' means the Commission established under this Act.

provisions.title: Section 3. Right to Information
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of this Act, all citizens shall have the right to information. Every citizen shall have access to information held by or under the control of public authorities. There shall be a general presumption in favour of disclosure: all information shall be disclosed unless it falls within an exemption specified in this Act. The right to information shall be exercised without giving reasons. Public authorities shall not require a citizen to state reasons for a request for information or to justify their interest in the information requested. The right to information shall be exercised consistent with the law and with good faith. Citizens exercising the right to information shall use the information obtained for lawful purposes.

provisions.title: Section 4. Proactive Disclosure
provisions.content: Every public authority shall proactively publish and widely disseminate information concerning all material decisions and activities under its control or in its possession. Information to be proactively published shall include: the details of its organisation, functions, and duties; the powers and duties of its officers and employees; the procedure followed in the decision-making process; the norms set by it for the discharge of its functions; the rules, regulations, instructions, manuals, and records held by it or under its control; a statement of the categories of documents held by it or under its control; the particulars of any arrangement that exists for consultation with, or representation by, the members of the public in relation to the formulation of its policy or implementation thereof; details of subsidies, programmes, or initiatives; a directory of its officers and employees; the budget allocated to each of its agencies; details of concessions, permits, or authorisations granted.

provisions.title: Section 5. Designation of Information Officers
provisions.content: Every public authority shall, within a reasonable time of the coming into force of this Act, designate as many officers as Central Information Officers or State Information Officers as may be necessary to provide information to persons requesting for information under this Act. Every public authority shall designate an officer at each sub-divisional level or other sub-district level to be a Central or State Assistant Information Officer to receive the applications for information or appeals and forward the same to the Central or State Information Officer. The officer designated under this section shall deal with requests from persons seeking information and render reasonable assistance to the persons seeking such information.

provisions.title: Section 6. Request for Obtaining Information
provisions.content: A person who desires to obtain any information under this Act shall make a request in writing or through electronic means in Bangla or English, accompanying such fee as may be prescribed, to the Central or State Information Officer. Where such request cannot be made in writing, the Information Officer shall render reasonable assistance to the person making the request orally to reduce the same in writing. The Information Officer on receipt of a request shall as expeditiously as possible, and in any case within thirty days of the receipt of the request, provide information to the applicant. In cases where the information requested for concerns the life or liberty of a person, the information shall be provided within forty-eight hours of the receipt of the request. A request for information shall not be refused solely because the request does not clearly specify the exact information sought.

provisions.title: Section 7. Exemptions from Disclosure
provisions.content: There shall be no obligation to give any citizen information including: information relating to national security and defence; information that would endanger the life or physical safety of any person; information relating to the relations between Bangladesh and any foreign state or international organisation; information likely to impede detection or apprehension of an offender; cabinet papers and records of deliberations of the Council of Ministers; information entrusted in confidence to public authorities; information relating to personal privacy; information affecting intellectual property rights; information affecting trade secrets or commercial confidence; and information prohibited from publication by any court of law. The list of exemptions shall be applied narrowly and in good faith, with doubts resolved in favour of disclosure. Exemptions based on harm to specific interests shall require a demonstration that disclosure would cause the specified harm.

provisions.title: Section 8. Partial Access
provisions.content: Where a request for access to information in a record also contains information that is exempt from disclosure, access may be provided to that part of the record that does not contain the exempt information and the applicant may be informed about the exemption applicable to the omitted portions. While providing the information under this clause, the public authority shall inform the person making the request about the non-exempt portion, the omission and the reason for the omission of the information. Access may be provided to the non-exempt portion irrespective of the fact that the exempt portion may constitute a larger proportion of the record. The public authority shall ensure that the non-exempt portion provided to the applicant does not reveal, directly or indirectly, information that is exempt from disclosure.

provisions.title: Section 9. Information Commission
provisions.content: The Government shall, by notification in the official Gazette, establish the Information Commission. The Information Commission shall be a body corporate and shall consist of a Chief Information Commissioner and not more than ten Information Commissioners. The Chief Information Commissioner shall be the head of the Information Commission. The Information Commission shall: receive and enquire into a complaint from any person who has not been able to submit an information request; receive and decide appeals filed against decisions of public authorities; monitor compliance of public authorities with the provisions of this Act; promote the right to information and develop the capacity of public authorities to implement the Act; advise the Government on the implementation of the Act; and prepare an annual report on the implementation of the Act.

provisions.title: Section 10. Appeals
provisions.content: Any person who does not receive a decision within the specified time or is aggrieved by a decision of the Information Officer may file an appeal with the appellate authority within thirty days from the expiry of such period or from the receipt of such a decision. The appellate authority shall dispose of the appeal within thirty days of the receipt of the appeal. A second appeal against the decision of the appellate authority shall lie with the Information Commission within ninety days from the date on which the decision should have been made or was actually made, whichever is later. On appeal, the Information Commission shall have the power to require the public authority to provide information; examine any record held by a public authority; impose penalties; and require the public authority to take disciplinary action against any officer.

provisions.title: Section 11. Record Management
provisions.content: Every public authority shall maintain its records duly catalogued and indexed in a manner and in the form which facilitates the right to information under this Act and ensure that all records that are appropriate to be computerised are, within a reasonable time and subject to availability of resources, computerised and connected through a network all over the country on different systems so that access to such records is facilitated. Records held by public authorities shall not be destroyed or removed without the consent of the competent authority. Public authorities shall maintain a schedule of records showing the categories of records held, their retention periods, and disposition procedures. The Government shall establish record management standards applicable to all public authorities.

provisions.title: Section 12. Fees for Information
provisions.content: A request for obtaining information shall be accompanied by an application fee as may be prescribed. Where the information officer decides to provide the information on payment of any further fee representing the cost of providing the information, the applicant shall be informed of the details of further fees required to be paid. The applicant may seek a review of the decision as to the amount of fees charged in the manner and circumstances as may be prescribed. Where access to the information is to be provided in printed or in any electronic format, the applicant shall pay such fee as may be prescribed. Information about the fee structure shall be published by each public authority and shall be prominently displayed at the public authority's offices and on its website.

provisions.title: Section 13. Penalties
provisions.content: Where the Information Commission at the time of deciding any complaint or appeal is of the opinion that the public authority has, without any reasonable cause, refused to receive an application for information or has not furnished information within the time specified or has, in bad faith, denied the request for information or knowingly given incorrect, incomplete, or misleading information, or destroyed information that was the subject of a request, the Information Commission shall impose a penalty on each such public information officer. The penalty shall be two hundred and fifty taka per day, subject to a maximum of twenty-five thousand taka, for each violation. The Information Commission shall also recommend disciplinary action against the responsible officer in cases of serious or repeated violations.

provisions.title: Section 14. Electronic Information Systems
provisions.content: Public authorities shall develop and maintain electronic information systems enabling the storage, retrieval, and disclosure of information in electronic format. Electronic information systems shall be designed in a manner facilitating compliance with proactive disclosure obligations and the timely processing of information requests. The Government shall develop standards for electronic information systems in public authorities, including requirements for interoperability, security, and accessibility. The Development and use of electronic information systems shall be planned to enable the progressive digitisation of public authority records and the provision of online access to public information. Public authorities shall implement appropriate security measures protecting their electronic information systems from unauthorised access and cyber threats.

provisions.title: Section 15. Implementation Responsibilities
provisions.content: The Ministry responsible for information affairs shall have primary responsibility for the implementation of this Act and shall: issue implementing regulations specifying fee schedules, record management standards, and other matters required by this Act; provide guidance to public authorities on their obligations under the Act; coordinate with the Information Commission on implementation matters; support the training of Information Officers; and report annually to Parliament on implementation progress. Each public authority shall prepare an annual report on its implementation of the Act and shall submit it to the Information Commission. The Information Commission shall consolidate these reports into a national annual report presented to Parliament.

definitions.definition: Any material in any form, including records, documents, memos, e-mails, opinions, and data material held in any electronic form.
Any authority, body, or institution established or constituted by or under the Constitution, by law, or by government notification.
The independent oversight body responsible for receiving complaints, deciding appeals, and monitoring compliance with the Right to Information Act.
An officer designated by a public authority to receive information requests and provide information to requesters.
The obligation of public authorities to publish and disseminate information without waiting for a specific request.
The right of every citizen to access information held by or under the control of a public authority.
